sources.name: UN Treaty Collection — XXI.10 status
sources._note: 2026-07-18: the previous URL used UPPERCASE /PAGES/, which ASP.NET routed to PageNotFound.aspx - a SOFT 404 returning HTTP 200 with an error page. A hash monitor would have reported it 'unchanged' indefinitely; only the redirect-and-title check caught it. Corrected to lowercase /pages/ for the citable human page, and monitoring now uses the official MTDSG status PDF - a stable URL giving clean change detection as ratifications are deposited. WHAT THIS CONCEALED: the BBNJ Agreement ENTERED INTO FORCE on 17 January 2026 (depositary notification C.N.447.2025.TREATIES-XXI.10). The source meant to surface exactly that could not have done so. VERIFIED 2026-07-18: the corpus is not affected - entry_into_force_date '2026-01-17' is already recorded across the BBNJ language records, captured by a human. The defect cost visibility, not accuracy. 2026-07-22: the lowercase /pages/ fix DID NOT WORK - the audit still saw a redirect to PageNotFound.aspx. The real cause was never the path case: ViewDetails.aspx routes to PageNotFound without a src=TREATY query parameter. Added src=TREATY (the canonical UN Treaty Collection form) and the page resolves. The monitor was unaffected throughout - it fetches monitor_url (the MTDSG PDF), re-verified live 2026-07-22: 'ENTRY INTO FORCE: 17 January 2026', Parties: 90. The human page is JS-rendered, so the audit can only redirect-check it, not content-anchor it (a stdlib fetch sees an empty shell).
